items.rationale: His central, most-recent focus: launched a Columbia research center for blockchain infrastructure and is building a YouTube series squarely on these layers, signaling where he sees the deepest open problems and durable value.

citations.quote: New research center focused on blockchain infrastructure @CUSEAS ! Made possible by @ethereumfndn. Super-excited about this
New YT series coming out later this year. But not much mechanism design (focused on consensus/execution/scaling)

items.rationale: Co-authored foundational research on DePIN, singling out the verification-of-services problem as the key challenge — treating it as an important emerging primitive worth foundational work.
items.age_note: Single substantive statement (2025-06-20), not restated since; treat as persisted into 2026.

citations.quote: New paper... developing some of the foundations for DePIN (decentralized physical infrastructure) networks, and specifically the key problem of verifying that the agreed-upon services/resources are indeed being provided

items.rationale: Repeatedly optimistic that SNARKs and even more exotic cryptographic primitives are becoming practical, viewing this as an enabling trend for the whole space.
items.age_note: Theme persists from 2024-12 to 2026-02; most recent (2026-02-11) reaffirms progress.

citations.quote: SNARKs for all of NP have come a long way since Kilian-Micali
optimism that cryptographic primitives that seem even crazier than SNARKs could be made practical in the coming decades

items.rationale: Co-authored a primer on arcade tokens with senior a16z colleagues, indicating engagement with consumer/gaming token design as a distinct emerging area.
items.age_note: One-time collaborative primer (2025-11-13); his role appears contributory rather than a strong personal thesis.

citations.quote: New primer on arcade tokens with my ever-amazing @a16zcrypto colleagues @skominers @eddylazzarin @milesjennings

items.rationale: Convenes and platforms work on Ethereum's execution layer, staking future, and interoperability; his center is funded by the Ethereum Foundation, anchoring his work to Ethereum's roadmap.

citations.quote: @dankrad @adietrichs on improving Ethereum's execution layer... @barnabemonnot @adietrichs @casparschwa @fradamt on the future of staking

items.rationale: Sustained technical interest in market design problems of crypto — LVR/AMMs, shared sequencing, execution tickets, Tullock contests — as rich, unsolved areas.
items.age_note: Heavy in 2024; 2026-03-18 explicitly de-emphasizes mechanism design for his upcoming series, suggesting a shift in personal focus toward consensus/execution/scaling.

citations.quote: My EC talk on Tullock contests in crypto/web3... Covers mining, staking, execution tickets, shared sequencing

items.rationale: Frames validator accountability when a chain stalls as an open, unsolved problem — implicitly a risk in current consensus designs he is working to address.

citations.quote: When a blockchain protocol stalls (i.e., has a liveness violation), which validators should be held accountable?

items.rationale: Co-authored an explanatory primer on arcade tokens, treating them as a token category worth defining — though framed educationally rather than as an investment call.
items.age_note: Single primer (2025-11-13); collaborative, not a standalone personal conviction.

citations.quote: New primer on arcade tokens with my ever-amazing @a16zcrypto colleagues

risk_regime.rationale: Roughgarden's corpus is almost entirely academic research, education, and team/center announcements — consensus protocols, cryptography, DePIN, mechanism design. He makes no statements about market direction, price, allocation, or risk appetite. His structural orientation is constructive on crypto infrastructure (especially Ethereum-aligned), but he expresses no tactical risk posture.

notes: Tim Roughgarden is a researcher/professor, not a markets-facing investor; his public statements are dominated by research papers, educational videos, the new Columbia blockchain infrastructure center (EF-funded), and team/intern announcements. Excitement entries reflect research conviction and intellectual focus, not trade theses, and conviction scores are calibrated accordingly. Most-recent signal (2026-03 statements) points to consensus/execution/scaling and cryptography (SNARKs) as his sharpest current focus, with a notable de-emphasis of mechanism design for his upcoming content series despite heavy earlier MEV/market-design work — recorded as a soft shift in age_notes. No token-level investment views and no risk-on/risk-off market posture are present in the corpus; risk_regime is no_view with high confidence in that classification.
